entries.title: How AI and satellites can help protect our oceans | Euronews Tech Talks
entries.description: The European Digital Twin Ocean builds on artificial intelligence and satellite technology to create a virtual replica of the ocean for scientists and policymakers. How does it work?

entries.title: Somali World Cup referee Omar Artan receives warm welcome home after being denied entry to US
entries.description: Andrew Giuliani, who is leading the White House task force for the FIFA World Cup, has said Artan was denied entry to the US for a "very good reason," without offering further details.

entries.title: Europe’s chaotic Entry/Exit System could take up to two years to stabilise, EU official warns
entries.description: Two months after Europe’s new biometric border checks launched, long queues and inconsistent procedures continue to frustrate travellers. Now, a Frontex official has warned that the troubled Entry/Exit System could take up to two years to fully stabilise.

entries.title: The world’s best hotel bars have just been named by Forbes – and 13 are in Europe
entries.description: From a bar in a former bank vault to a spot serving up caviar chicken nuggets, these hotel bars are a cut above the rest.

entries.title: Defying Russia: Poland and Germany plan massive offshore wind farms in the Baltic Sea
entries.description: Despite hybrid attacks from Russia, Poland is betting on offshore wind power from the Baltic Sea. At a forum in Berlin, one message was clear: the region could become Europe’s next major energy hub. Will Europe seize this opportunity?

entries.title: Netherlands: Marktweg in The Hague becomes iconic orange street for World Cup fever
entries.description: Residents of Marktweg in The Hague have once again transformed their street into a sea of orange ahead of the FIFA World Cup, continuing a popular local tradition that has become a symbol of community spirit and support for the national team.

entries.title: Madrid’s Nobu Hotel to open in September with three-storey restaurant from famed Japanese chef
entries.description: Spain’s luxury hotel scene is set for a major addition this September with the grand opening of Nobu Hotel Madrid. Opening ahead of the Formula 1 weekend, the design-led stay promises skyline views, signature Chef Nobu Matsuhisa dishes and a prime location in the capital.

entries.title: ‘Europe cannot afford bureaucratic paralysis’: EU strikes defence simplification deal
entries.description: Facing Russian aggression and US retreat, the EU is planning to streamline permits, cut bureaucracy and make defence procurement and transfers across member states easier.

entries.title: Germany's most expensive hotels: luxury stays up to €53,000 a night
entries.description: In Germany's most exclusive hotels, a night costs up to 53,000 euros. These seven suites are setting new standards in comfort, service and prestige.

entries.title: Philippines continues rescue effort after powerful Mindanao quake
entries.description: The Philippines continued rescue operations on 10 June after a magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck Mindanao, killing at least 37 people, injuring nearly 500 and forcing more than 32,000 residents from their homes.

entries.title: One dead and two missing in missile strike on tanker near Oman
entries.description: The Palau-flagged Settebello transmitted a distress call on Wednesday reporting a missile strike on its engine room off Sohar in the Gulf of Oman, where local authorities are assisting with crew evacuation, the UK maritime agency reported.

entries.title: Thai woman in custody after US diplomat found dead in Myanmar, State Department says
entries.description: The Thai Embassy in Yangon and Thailand's Foreign Ministry refused to say whether they had provided consular support to the suspect or give any other information.

entries.title: These are all the carriers banned from flying in Europe after EU Air Safety List update
entries.description: With the EU’s latest Air Safety List including Air Express Algeria among carriers banned from operating within European airspace, here’s what travellers need to know about how airlines end up on it.

entries.title: Subway station in Mexico City goes viral over chandeliers
entries.description: Decorative chandeliers installed at a busy Mexico City subway station ahead of the 2026 World Cup have sparked a wave of memes and mixed reactions from commuters.

entries.title: A turning point: Why Germany’s military capabilities are underestimated
entries.description: Many people believe that Germans are not prepared for defence. In a guest article for Euronews, military sociologist Dr Timo Graf uses recent survey data to show why this view underestimates German society’s capacity for defence.

entries.title: 'Europe should evolve its carbon market, not dilute it,' investors say
entries.description: A group of investors managing €11.4 trillion in funds are advocating for complementary industrial policies such as grids, electrification and clean tech financing, reflecting a broader shift in European climate policy from setting targets toward managing industrial transformation.

entries.title: Julian Barnes quits fiction after winning 2026 Princess of Asturias for literature
entries.description: The British novelist said at a recent launch of his essay 'Farewells' at Barcelona's Caixafòrum that this would be his final novel, after turning 80 and spending over 45 years devoted to literature.

entries.title: Trump vows fresh attacks on Iran and accuses negotiators of 'playing us for suckers'
entries.description: The US launched air strikes against Iran early on Wednesday and Tehran fired back at countries in the region in escalating attacks that threatened to derail efforts to end the war.

entries.title: UK: Belfast violence leaves homes burnt and families displaced
entries.description: Belfast awoke to scenes of destruction on 10 June after anti immigration protests descended into violence across several parts of Northern Ireland, leaving homes damaged, vehicles burnt out and families displaced.

entries.title: Five EU countries push tougher safeguards for future members to avoid Orbán repeat
entries.description: Germany, France, the Netherlands, Belgium and Luxembourg want stronger safeguards to act faster against democratic and legal breaches in future EU members. The proposal reflects the lessons learned from the Orbán era.

entries.title: Kyiv hit Russian military plant using Ukrainian-made Flamingo missile, Zelenskyy says
entries.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy vowed to continue Kyiv’s strikes deep inside Russia in response to attacks on Ukraine, but also a way of forcing the Kremlin into direct talks.

entries.title: Hong Kong charges seven people and two firms over massive fire that killed 168
entries.description: The massive blaze, which engulfed seven of the eight high-rise apartment blocks at the Wang Fuk Court estate in November, prompted a months-long investigation into the cause.

entries.title: Deadly clashes in Kenya as protesters fight US Ebola quarantine facility
entries.description: Kenya saw fresh unrest on 9 June as protesters defied a court order and clashed with police near Laikipia Air Base over plans for a US funded Ebola quarantine facility, fuelling fears about the virus and prompting arrests.

entries.title: Multiple attackers kill 12 people in late-night shooting in South Africa, police say
entries.description: Police said the motive was not yet known and no arrests had been made, but there were suspicions it was related to competition over illegal mining in the area.

entries.title: Patrick Bruel scandal: French prosecutors seek charges and pre-trial detention over sex abuse claims
entries.description: After 48 hours in custody, the Nanterre prosecutor on Wednesday sought formal charges and pre-trial detention for the 67-year-old artist, accused of multiple sexual assaults he vehemently denies.

entries.title: Euronews Culture's Film of the Week: 'Disclosure Day' - Close Encounters Of The Fourth Kind?
entries.description:
Steven Spielberg revisits his passion for interstellar visitors, with a government conspiracy begging to be blown wide open... 
NB: Spoiler-free review.

entries.title: Pakistan army helicopter crashes in Kashmir after technical fault, killing all on board
entries.description: Witnesses said that the helicopter crashed shortly after take-off from a helipad. Ambulances arrived at the scene and transported the victims to a nearby hospital.

entries.title: Trust in France's institutions 'at stake' after girl's killing, Emmanuel Macron says
entries.description: The body of the girl, named as Lyhanna, was found last week after she went missing on 29 May in the southwestern town of Fleurance.

entries.title: UN atomic watchdog demands urgent cooperation from Iran and access to nuclear sites
entries.description: The resolution comes at a time of heightened tensions in the Middle East, with the US having launched airstrikes against Iran early on Wednesday and Tehran firing back at countries in the region.

entries.title: 'Partners and friends’: Trade and defence top of agenda at EU-South Korea summit
entries.description: As security threats grow and geopolitics shift, the European Union and Republic of South Korea have celebrated a new Digital Trade Agreement at a summit in Brussels.

entries.title: Long-term fertiliser solutions needed to avoid EU food shortages, EU Agriculture Commissioner says
entries.description: The Agriculture and Food Commissioner warned that, to avoid looming food shortages in the face of rising fertiliser costs, the bloc must secure sustainable fertiliser supplies and not just provide short-term financial support.

entries.title: 'We have already given enough': Bulgaria's PM says weapons supplies to Ukraine will end
entries.description: Bulgaria, which is member of NATO and the European Union, has been providing military aid Ukraine since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in 2022.

entries.title: ‘Amaterasu’ performance marks 160 years of Belgium-Japan relations in Brussels
entries.description: The Japanese performance Amaterasu was staged at Brussels Town Hall as part of celebrations marking the 160th anniversary of relations between Belgium and Japan. Organised by Be-Japon, the event showcased Japanese heritage through kimono fashion and brought together cultural enthusiasts.

entries.title: Trump says 'I love the inflation,' claims US taking millions of barrels of Iranian oil
entries.description: President Donald Trump welcomed new inflation data showing prices rising 4.2%, saying the numbers were "great." He argued higher energy costs linked to the Iran conflict were temporary and claimed US forces were moving millions of barrels of oil through the Strait of Hormuz.

entries.title: 'Children are dying,' Cuba says as US blockade hampers delivery of UN aid
entries.description: The blockade, coupled with expanded US sanctions that punish companies doing business with the Cuban state, has intensified the worst economic and energy crisis on the island in over a generation.

entries.title: Infantino defends World Cup ticket prices, says fans 'should chill' about ref denied US entry
entries.description: The 2026 men's World Cup has been expanded to 48 teams and is the biggest and most ambitious in the event's history, with matches taking place in Mexico, Canada and the United States.

entries.title: Somalia's World Cup referee returns home to hero's welcome after US entry denial

entries.title: Why are World Cup tickets so expensive?
entries.description: An expert says strong demand, dynamic pricing and a booming resale market are pushing World Cup ticket prices higher. As teams advance and knockout matchups become clearer, prices could continue to fluctuate based on fan interest and demand.

entries.description: The World Cup is on, the single biggest sport event of the year, followed by millions of people around the world. So far, the vibes are strangely subdued. Ticket sales and hotel bookings are below expectations and immigration issues are making headlines - especially in the US.

entries.title: US and Iran trade strikes for second day, threatening return of all-out war
entries.description: The new US assault across multiple Iranian cities came as efforts to negotiate an end the war again appeared stuck, with Iran insisting it would maintain its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz, which has disrupted global energy supplies and sent oil prices higher.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 11th, 2026 – Morning

entries.title: From Budapest’s famous baths to Alton Towers: The world’s most disappointing tourist attractions
entries.description: Bucket-list attractions don't always deliver. entries.title: From Japan With Love: London exhibition explores how NIGO reshaped fashion, music and hype culture
entries.description: The exhibition, NIGO: From Japan With Love, brings together over 700 objects from his personal archive, charting his evolution from teenage Americana obsessive to global designer and artistic director of Kenzo.

We speak exclusively to Glenn Micallef, the EU Commissioner for Sports to hear the Brussels view on one of the most political World Cups to date.

entries.title: Thousands protest demanding Albania's PM Rama resigns

entries.title: The EU’s Digital Markets Act: Fines, delayed features and unclear benefits

entries.description: Ten years following the landmark vote, the EU's former Brexit negotiator said the bloc's door is open to the UK, but that London "cannot cherry-pick" EU policies.

entries.title: US Supreme Court clears way for mass deportations of Haitians and Syrians
entries.description: The 6-3 ruling hands Donald Trump a sweeping victory on immigration, stripping legal protections from hundreds of thousands of people the State Department warns would be returning to dangerous countries.

entries.title: Death toll from Venezuela earthquakes rises to 235, search and rescue efforts continue
entries.description: The two quakes struck in quick succession, just minutes apart, according to the US Geological Survey, marking it one of the South American nation’s worst disasters in over a century.

entries.title: Europe’s Taliban Dilemma: the struggle to return Afghan migrants
entries.description: EU countries are engaging with Afghanistan’s Taliban-led regime to return irregular migrants deemed security threats or convicted of serious offences. Yet any such arrangement could carry political and diplomatic costs, requiring concessions and risking public backlash.

entries.title: Côte d’Ivoire: Discover West Africa’s beach and culture hotspot
entries.description: Regarded as West Africa’s answer to the Caribbean, Côte d’Ivoire has an extraordinary coast, UNESCO-listed culture and direct flights from Europe.

entries.title: Gulf states weigh living side by side with Iran as Tehran weaponises Hormuz
entries.description: As the frontline countries paying the largest price for the Iran war, pressed by severe economic urgencies, the Gulf states are beginning their own firm process towards regional stability with rival Iran, while Tehran continues weaponising the Strait of Hormuz to sow division with its neighbours.

entries.title: Euronews Culture's Film of the Week: 'Supergirl' - Save the puppy, save the world
entries.description: After a strong start to the rebooted DC Universe last year with 'Superman' comes  a new space adventure which follows the Man of Steel's chaotic cousin... And her super-powered pup.

entries.title: How Portugal's Alentejo region has become a hotspot for luxury hotel openings
entries.description: New openings, villa relaunches and stylish boutique stays are drawing fresh attention to Portugal’s Alentejo region, from Comporta and Melides to the historic streets of Évora.

entries.title: EU releases €3 billion loan for Ukraine as US-Iran talks remain fragile
entries.description: Ukraine has received 3.2 billion euros as the first disbursement under the European Union's 90 billion euro support loan. Meanwhile, Iranians are rejecting claims that the UN would visit nuclear enrichment sites in the country.

entries.title: Harvey Weinstein's New York rape charge dropped - accuser says she can't endure a fourth trial
entries.description: Weinstein still stands convicted of another sexual felony in New York and others in California. He remains behind bars at New York's maximum-security prison, Rikers Island.

entries.title: Newsletter: Temperatures rise over sanctions against Russia

entries.title: Historic joy in Abidjan as Ivory Coast reach World Cup knockouts for first time
entries.description: Ivory Coast reaches World Cup knockout stage for the first time after Nicolas Pépé’s brace beats Curaçao 2-0, sparking huge celebrations in Abidjan.

entries.title: UK foreign policy to remain unchanged despite government transition, deputy PM Lammy says
entries.description: The UK's deputy Prime Minister David Lammy has told Europe Today that his country's foreign policy — including its support for Ukraine and efforts to restore EU ties — will remain unchanged when a new Prime Minister takes the reins after Keir Starmer's exit.

entries.title: Turkey produces last minute winner to beat the US as Germany end win streak with loss to Ecuador
entries.description: The final matchday saw teams take on both more cautious and reckless approaches as they looked to secure qualification into the knockout stages.

entries.title: Tehran will allow nuclear inspections during peace talks, former Iranian diplomat says
entries.description: Seyed Hossein Mousavian, former spokesman for Iran's nuclear negotiation team, tells Euronews he believes Tehran will allow the IAEA access to its nuclear sites as part of negotiations with the US, despite some Iranian officials saying access could only be granted after a final deal has been struck.

entries.title: Amid shifts in energy supply, which EU countries consume and produce the most gas?

entries.title: Peppa Pig backlash as US company Hasbro requires child actors to sign voices over to AI
entries.description: US entertainment giant Hasbro is reportedly asking child actors on the popular series Peppa Pig to sign over their voices to artificial intelligence under new contract terms. An open letter signed by 1,000 industry professionals condemns the AI contract clause and warns of matters of consent.

entries.title: Podcast | From Nicosia to Dublin: Cyprus to pass the baton to Ireland

entries.title: Venezuela earthquakes: bodies recovered as rescue efforts continue in La Guaira

entries.title: EU ban on Russian combatants faces resistance from France and Italy
entries.description: Rome and Paris have cited legal and technical concerns over how the ban should be applied in practice.

entries.description: Thousands of Shiite pilgrims mark Ashoura in Karbala, mourning Imam Hussein’s 680 AD death with processions, prayers and reinforced security.

entries.description: Inspectors discovered an additional 2,100 tonnes of textile waste stored in a warehouse linked to a Turkish recycling facility that was noncompliant with environmental laws. Meanwhile, France is urging EU countries to get tough on ultra-fast fashion.
